compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
`canonical_tracker` (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:81-84`, `os.path.realpath`) is the lock-path
anchor that guarantees a symlinked-path caller and a real-path caller contend on the **same**
`.ticket-write.lock` / `.ticket-write.lock.d` (`acquire` canonicalizes at `lock.py:289`).
VALIDATED: zero test references `canonical_tracker`; the invariant is untested.

## What
Add a test proving that two independent acquirers — one addressing the tracker by its real
path, the other by a symlink to it — mutually exclude, in BOTH directions.

Determinism (no sleep race): the two acquirers are two independent `acquire()` calls, each
opening its OWN file descriptor (fcntl leg) and attempting its OWN `os.mkdir` (mkdir leg).
Per the Linux `flock(2)` contract, separate `open()` descriptions to the same file contend
even within one process, and `os.mkdir` fails `EEXIST` on an existing dir regardless of
process — so holding the lock via one path makes the second acquirer via the OTHER path
deterministically fail. The second acquirer uses `timeout=1, attempts=1` (a bounded 1s
budget); because the lock is genuinely held, it is GUARANTEED to exhaust that budget and
raise `LockTimeout` — a deterministic outcome, not a timing race that a slow runner could
flip. Teeth: were `canonical_tracker` not resolving the symlink, the two paths would key
different lock files, the second acquire would SUCCEED, and the `LockTimeout` assertion would
fail — so the test genuinely exercises the canonicalization invariant.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py` — add the symlink/realpath lock-identity tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Holding the lock acquired via the REAL path, a second `acquire()` via a symlink to the
      tracker raises `LockTimeout` (blocked); after release, the symlink caller acquires.
- [ ] The reverse direction (hold via the symlink, contend via the real path) also raises
      `LockTimeout`, then succeeds after release.
- [ ] Both tests are deterministic (bounded `timeout=1, attempts=1`; the block is guaranteed
      by a genuinely-held lock, not by sleep timing).
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: canonical_tracker invariant under test; reference only

compiled_state.title: Add canonical_tracker symlink/realpath cross-process lock-identity test
